original: The last Queen of Cyprus, Catherine Cornaro is forced to abdicate by Venice
Storm floods ravage Gorinchem, Dordrecht & Woudrichem, Netherlands
England grants a patent to Providence Plantations for what is now Rhode Island
Thirty Years' War: Bavaria, Cologne, France and Sweden sign the Truce of Ulm
First American town meeting is held in Boston's Faneuil Hall
On board HMS Monarch (his own flagship), British Admiral John Byng is executed by firing squad for failing to come to aide of besieged British garrison
Lieutenant Governor Simcoe of Upper Canada passes legislation for the first time against the importation of slaves, after a black female slave forcibly transported to New York state to be sold
US Congress authorizes war bonds to finance War of 1812
African Methodist Episcopal Zion Church is founded in New York
General Congress of South American States assembles in Panama
José Zorrilla's romantic drama "El Zapatero y el Rey" premieres in Madrid
-5.3°F (-20.7°C) in Groningen, Netherlands
Battle of New Bern North Carolina: General Burnside conquers New Bern
Union troops occupy Fort DeRussy, Louisiana
Defeat of Maori Ngāti Ruanui leader Riwha Titokowaru in Taranaki, New Zealand by British forces
California legislature approves act making Golden Gate Park possible
Second largest snowfall in NYC history (21")
August Strindberg's play "Froken Julie" premieres in Copenhagen
Sutro Baths in San Francisco opens by Cliff House (closed Sept 1, 1952)
Stanley Cup, Montreal Arena, Westmount, Quebec: Montreal Shamrocks beat Queens University, 6-2 to clinch trophy for CAHL
US currency goes on the gold standard after Congress passes the Currency Act
Germany's Chancellor von Bulow declares that the agreement Germany signed with Great Britain in October 1900, to restrain foreign aggression and maintain open trade, does not apply to Manchuria
1st national bird reservation established in Sebastian, Florida
Stanley Cup, Dey's Arena, Ottawa, Ontario: Ottawa HC beats Rat Portage Thisles, 4-2 for a 2-0 challenge series sweep
W.B. Yeats & Lady Gregory's play "Hour-glass" premieres in Dublin
In a landmark case, Northern Securities Company v United States, the US Supreme Court finds the company has violated the Sherman Anti-Trust Act; first case in T. Roosevelt's 'trust-busting' campaign
By Presidential order, Japanese laborers are excluded from entering the USA
Stanley Cup, Montreal Arena, Westmount, Quebec: Montreal Wanderers beat Toronto Professionals, 6-4
Amsterdam Social-Democratic Party (SDP) forms
Lakeview Gusher, the largest U.S. oil well gusher near Bakersfield, California, vented to atmosphere
King Vittorio Emanuel III of Italy injured in an assassination attempt

South Africa's Supreme Court declares that marriages not celebrated according to Christian rites or not registered by the Registrar of Marriages are invalid; all Muslim and Hindu marriages are therefore declared invalid
Serbia & Turkey sign peace treaty
German cruiser Dresden scuttled off Más a Tierra, Chile, having been pursued by the Royal Navy after the Battle of the Falkland Islands, with her engines worn out and virtually no coal
Battle of Verdun: Germans capture Cumières-le-Mort-Homme and Chattancourt in France
1st concrete ship to cross the Atlantic (Faith) is launched in San Francisco
KGU-AM in Honolulu HI begins radio transmissions
KSD-AM in Saint Louis MO begins radio transmissions
WGR-AM in Buffalo NY begins radio transmissions
Allies accepts Vilnius taking East-Galicia in Poland
German Supreme Court prohibits NSDAP (Nazi party)
Train in Costa Rica falls into the Río Virilla, killing 248 and injuring 93
First theater built for rear movie projection in NYC
US Civilian Conservation Corp begins tree conservation program
36-Folsom becomes 1st line to use 1-man streetcars in San Francisco
Federal Register, 1st magazine of the US government, publishes 1st issue
England draw with South Africa at Durban on the 10th day
Nazi Germany dissolves Republic of Czechoslovakia
27 killed, 15 injured when truck full of migrant workers collides with a train outside McAllen, Texas
Nazi occupiers of Holland forbid Jewish owned companies
Xavier Cugat & His Orchestra record "Babalu"
RAF bomb cuts railway link Hannover-Hamm
Freedom Train arrives in San Francisco
FBI starts a public list of the Ten Most Wanted Fugitives
Earthquake at Euskirchen, Germany
KOLR TV channel 10 in Springfield, MO (CBS) begins broadcasting
KDAL (now KDLH) TV channel 3 in Duluth-Superior, MN (CBS) begins
Prince Mahemdra becomes king of Nepal
Indonesian government of Sastroamidjojo resigns
Recording Industry Association of American created
RIAA certifies 1st gold record - Perry Como's single "Catch A Falling Star"
South Africa's government prohibits the African National Congress
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
USSR performs atmospheric nuclear test
14 die in a train crash in Bakersfield, California
Disarmament conference opens in Geneva without France
Israeli cabinet approves diplomatic relations with West Germany
1967 NFL Draft: Michigan State defensive end Bubba Smith first pick by Baltimore Colts
CBS TV suspends Radio Free Europe free advertising because RFE doesn't make it clear it is sponsored by the CIA
Barbara Jo Rubin becomes first female jockey to win at Aqueduct Racetrack, NYC aboard 2-year-old bay Bravy Galaxy at 13 to 1
CBS-TV renews "The Smothers Brothers Comedy Hour" for a third season
West Indies cricket batsman Seymour Nurse scores career high 258 in his last Test innings in 3rd Test win over NZ at Christchurch
Ursula K. Le Guin is the first woman to win the Nebula Award for Best Novel with her work "The Left Hand of Darkness"
South Vietnamese troops flee Laos
NBA's Cincinnati Royals, plagued by poor home attendance, announce they are moving franchise to Kansas City
Two IRA members shot dead by British soldiers in the Bogside area of Derry
Liam Cosgrave (52) appointed President of Ireland
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Marines terminate Moluccan action in Province house (1 dead)
NFL permanently adds 7th official (side judge)
Hawker Siddeley Trident plane crashes into a factory near Beijing, China, killing at least 200
Ice Dance Championship at Dortmund West Germany won by Regoczy & Sallay
Ice Pairs Championship at Dortmund won by Cherkasova & Shakhrai (USSR)
Polish airliner crash kills all 87 aboard (22 are US amateur boxers)
Worlds Ladies' Figure Skating Champ in Dortmund won by Anett Potzsch
NCAA St Joseph's upsets top seed DePaul 49-48
OPEC cut oil prices for 1st time in 23 years
Challenger moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida), for mating of STS 41-C mission
Michael Secrest (US) completes 24-hr ride of 516 miles, 427 yards
European Space Agency's Giotto flies by Halley's Comet (605 km)
Providence, with Billy Donovan's 25 points, beats Austin Peay 90-87
Skier Piotr Fijas jumps record 194m
Worlds Ladies' Figure Skating Champ in Cincinnati won by Katarina Witt
Emir of Kuwait returns to Kuwait City, after the Iraqis leave
English Court of Appeal frees "Birmingham 6" who had been unjustly sentenced in August 1975 to life imprisonment
Ice Dance Championship at Munich won by Isabel & Phil Duchesnay (FRA)
Ice Pairs Championship at Munich won by N Mishkutenok & A Dmitriev
Soviet newspaper "Pravda" suspends publication
3,000th performance of "Nunsense"
Johan Koss skates world record 5km (6:36.57)
Mexican banker and billionaire Alfredo Harp Helu kidnapped
Soyuz TM-21 launches with V Dezyurov, G Strekalov & N Thagard
Timeline of Linux development: Linux kernel version 1.0.0 is released
1st time 13 people in space
Crufts show at NEC Birmingham, (1995 winner, Joshua, an Irish setter)
68-year-old Gordie Howe signs AHL contract with Syracuse Crunch
Iranian military plane crashes, killing 80
Olympic gold medalist Michael Johnson wins 67th James E Sullivan Award
The Chinese city of Chongqing (formerly Chunking) is upgraded to a centrally administered municipality
Start of weekend of protests against war in Iraq that are attended by millions

Cedar Revolution, where over a million Lebanese march in the streets of Beirut to demonstrate against the Syrian military presence in Lebanon, and against the government, following the assassination of Prime Minister Rafik Hariri.

Canterbury winger Hazem El Masri becomes highest point scorer in Australian Rugby League history; 33rd minute penalty in Bulldogs' 34-12 win over Manly at ANZ Stadium, Sydney takes him to 2,178; Andrew Johns, 2,176
25 people are killed and 50 are wounded by a series of car bombings in Baghdad, Iraq
7 people are killed after gunmen storm a bar in Cancun, Mexico
NASA releases data showing February 2016 warmest month ever recorded globally - 1.35C above the long-term average
European Court of Justice rules companies can ban staff from wearing religious symbols, including headscarves
World's oldest golf club, Muirfield in Scotland, votes to admit women as members for the first time in 273 years
Brazilian human rights politician Marielle Franco is murdered in Rio, prompting mass protests
UK announces it will expel 23 Russian diplomats after Russian-made nerve agent used on former spy in UK
US students across American commemorate Florida high school shooting with mass walkouts across the country
World Happiness Report names Finland as world's happiest country and Burundi the unhappiest
California officially free of drought for the first time in more than 7 years (Dec 2011)
Former US Democratic representative Beto O'Rourke announces he is running for president
Google announces its employee Emma Haruka Iwao has broken the world record for calculating pi, to 31.4 trillion digits, on pi day using Google Cloud
Tropical Cyclone Idai comes ashore in Mozambique, killing at least 417 people, and Malawi killing at least 56, after causing widespread flooding
Longest-ever scheduled passenger flight by distance is flown by Air Tahiti Nui from French Polynesia to mainland France, covering 9,765 miles (15,715 km) as a domestic flight due to COVID-19
Myanmar opposition leader Mahn Win Khaing Than calls for a revolution "This is the darkest moment of the nation and the moment that the dawn is close," as protester death toll over 120
UK police officer charged with the death of Sarah Everard, who disappeared walking home in south London, and whose death sparked debate about violence against women
Civilians able to leave heavily bombed Ukranian city of Mariupol for the first time, amid dead toll of 2,500 in the city and a humanitarian crisis
Omicron causes the largest outbreak of COVID-19 in China since Wuhan 2020 with 26 million people under lockdown in Changchun and Shenzhen
Argentina's annual inflation rate pasts 100% at 102.5%, for the first time since hyperinflation in 1991, one of the highest in the world, according to its statistics agency
Czech free diver David Vencl completes record dive beneath ice to 52.1 meters, without a wetsuit, in Switzerland’s Lake Sils in temperatures between 1C and 4C (34F to 39F)
The US, UK and Australia unveil details of their AUKUS deal to create a fleet of nuclear-powered submarines to be based in the Indo Pacific region to counter Chinese military build-up
US large MQ-9 Reaper drone crashes into the Black Sea after collision with Russian fighter jet, with the US saying the Russia forced it down sparking an international incident
A series of storms strike parts of Ohio, Kentucky, Indiana and Arkansas, with tornadoes leaving at least 40 injured and three people dead
At least 60 migrants die from dehydration and hunger with 25 rescued after their dinghy engine breaks down off the coast of Libya
HMS Tyger, a sunken British warship that ran aground in 1742, is identified lying off Garden Key, Dry Tortugas National Park
Philippine police rescue over 500 people from a scam centre north of Manila, forced to pose as lovers online to defraud people
A severe storm system begins across the US South and Midwest bringing high winds, dust storms and more than 60 tornadoes over two days, killing at least 42 people
Battle of Montiel:Peter of Castile(Peter the Cruel) with support from England is defeated by an alliance between the French and his half-brotherHenry II of Castile
Convention of the Estates of Scotland opens to decide on offering Scottish Crown toWilliam IIIandMary Stuartor toJames IIof England
Eli Whitneypatents the cotton gin, revolutionizing the cotton industry in the southern US states
Luigi Chiaramonti electedPope Pius VII
Henry Addingtonbecomes Prime Minister of the United Kingdom after his friendWilliam Pitt the Youngerresigns after being unable to persuade KingGeorge IIIof the need for Catholic Emancipation
Premiere ofGiuseppe Verdi's opera "Macbeth" at the Teatro della Pergola, Florence
Seventh Day Adventist co-founderEllen G. Whitereceives a vision while attending a funeral service in Lovett's Grove, near Bowling Green, Ohio
Gioachino Rossini's sacred work "Petite Messe Solennelle" premieres at his home in Paris, France
Bedřich Smetana's symphonic poem "Vysehrad" / "The High Castle" premieres in Prague, Kingdom of Bohemia; it later becomes the first section of the "Má vlast" collection of tone poems
W. S. GilbertandArthur Sullivan's comic opera "The Mikado" premieres in London at the Savoy Theatre
GermanFerdinand von Zeppelinreceives a US patent for a "Navigable Balloon"
Dutch botanist Hugo de Vries rediscoversGregor Mendel's laws of heredity and genetics
John D. Rockefellergives $100 million to Rockefeller Foundation
US PresidentWarren G. Hardingbecomes 1st president to pay taxes
James F. Hanley, Joseph Meyer, and Buddy DeSylva's musical "Big Boy", starringAl Jolson, closes  at the Winter Garden Theatre, NYC, after 56 performances
Battle of the Century: US comediansFred AllenandJack Bennymeet on radio during their "feud"
PopePius XIpublishes anti-nazi-encyclical Mit brennender Sorge
"The Road to Singapore" directed by Victor Schertzinger starringBob Hope,Bing Crosby, and Dorothy Lamour, first of seven such films, premieres
American film noir classic "Gilda", starringRita Hayworthand Glenn Ford, and directed by Charles Vidor, premieres in New York City
Bess Fosburgh Kaiser, wife ofHenry J. Kaiser, dies
Milwaukee Braves future home run kingHank Aaronhomers in his debut exhibition game against the Boston Red Sox
50-year-old baseball pitching starSatchel Paigesigns a contract to play for and manage the Birmingham Black Barons of the Negro National League
Philadelphia centerWilt Chamberlainsets NBA playoff record of 53 points in Warriors' 132-112 win over Syracuse Nationals at Philadelphia Civic Center
Former New York Yankees general managerGeorge Weissbecomes first President of New York Mets after MLB expansion franchise formed
Red Wings' forwardGordie Howebecomes second player in NHL history to score 500 career goals in Detroit's 3-2 loss to NY Rangers

MusicianFrank Zappa(22) tapes an appearance for syndicated "TheSteve AllenShow" - he and the host 'play' bicycles as musical instruments along with the show's orchestra; program airs later in the month
Dallas, Texas;Jack Rubysentenced to death forLee Harvey Oswald's murder
British film "Born Free" based on the book "Born Free" byJoy Adamsonreleased starring Virginia McKenna and Bill Travers
JFK's body moved from temporary grave to a permanent memorial
Barbra Streisandappears on "TheBurt BacharachSpecial" on CBS TV
The Rolling Stonesleave England for France in an attempt to escape taxes
14th Grammy Awards:Carole King's "It's Too Late";Bill Withers' "Ain't No Sunshine"; Carly Simon;Isaac Hayes;Muddy Waters; and Bill Evans win
Muddy Waterswins his first Grammy Award, for his album"They Call Me Muddy Waters"
Future US senatorJohn McCainis released after spending over five years in a North Vietnamese prisoner of war camp
Film adaptation ofNeil Simon's comedy "The Prisoner of Second Avenue", starringJack LemmonandAnne Bancroft, premieres in NYC
American jockeyBill Shoemakerwins his 7,000th race
Gerry Adams, head of Sinn Féin, is seriously wounded in an assassination attempt in central Belfast
NY MetDarryl Strawberrycharges Red Sox pitcher Al Nipper during spring training exhibition game, causes bench clearing brawl
Mikhail Gorbachevbecomes president of the Soviet Congress
TheDave MatthewsBand perform their first show as part of a benefit for the Middle East Children's Alliance, in Charlottesville, Virginia
NY Met Daryl Boston, Vince Coleman &Dwight Goodenaccused of rape
Ricky Pontinghits twin tons for Tasmania aged 18 years 84 days
US PresidentBill Clintontrips and injures his knee requiring surgery
20th Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees: Buddy Guy; The O'Jays;The Pretenders; Percy Sledge;U2; Frank Barsalona; and Seymour Stein
Mike Wallaceretires from US news program "60 Minutes" after 37 years
26th Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees:Alice CooperBand;Neil Diamond;Dr. John; Darlene Love;Tom Waits; Leon Russell; Jac Holzman; and Art Rupe
Xi Jinpingis named President of the People's Republic of China
Marco Rubioannounces he is dropping out of the Republican presidential nomination race
PresidentVladimir Putinorders Russian troops out of Syria
Angela Merkelsworn in for fourth term as German Chancellor, head of a coalition government, 171 days after the general election
NASA twin study finds thatScott Kellyis no longer identical to his twin brother after one year in space, 7% of his genes altered
US Senate passes resolution overturning PresidentDonald Trump's national emergency declaration
63rd Grammy Awards:Beyoncébreaks record for most Grammys won by a singer (28),Taylor Swiftwins Album of the Year andBillie EilishRecord of the Year
Dolly Partonremoves herself from the ballot for the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame saying "I don’t feel that I have earned that right” (she later reconsiders and gets in)
Mark Carneyis sworn in as Canada's new Prime Minister, replacingJustin Trudeauafter nine years
